items.titleZh: 图变换器加速集成电路互连信号完整性分析（布法罗、斯图加特、IBM）
items.descriptionZh:
近日，来自纽约州立大学布法罗分校、斯图加特大学以及IBM Research的研究团队联合发表了一篇题为《SI-GT: Fast Interconnect Signal Integrity Analysis For Integrated Circuit Design Via Graph Transformers》的论文，提出了一种基于图变换器（Graph Transformer）的新型模型，用于实现集成电路（IC）互连中快速且准确的信号完整性（SI）分析。该研究成果于2026年4月发布在《半导体工程》（Semiconductor Engineering）上，为先进工艺节点下日益严峻的互连信号质量挑战提供了全新的解决方案。

在深亚微米及更先进制程中，互连线的寄生效应（如电阻、电容、电感）以及耦合噪声、反射、串扰等信号完整性问题，已成为制约芯片性能、功耗和可靠性的关键瓶颈。传统的信号完整性分析方法，如基于电磁场求解器的全波仿真或SPICE级瞬态模拟，虽然精度高，但计算开销极大，无法满足大规模设计迭代和快速签核的需求。近年来，机器学习方法被尝试用于加速SI分析，但大多受限于特征提取的局部性和模型泛化能力不足。

SI-GT模型的核心创新在于将信号完整性问题转化为图结构上的学习任务。研究人员将IC互连网络建模为图（Graph），其中节点代表互连线段、过孔、晶体管引脚等关键物理单元，边则表征彼此之间的电气耦合（如容性串扰、感性耦合）和拓扑连接关系。在此基础上，模型引入图变换器架构，利用自注意力机制（Self-Attention）动态捕捉全局范围内的长程依赖交互，从而更准确地预测每个节点或边上的信号完整性指标，例如峰值串扰噪声、信号延迟偏差和过冲/下冲幅度。

相比传统逐段建模或仅考虑局部邻域的图神经网络（GNN），图变换器能够通过多头注意力聚合远距离互连之间的非局部耦合效应，这对于当前高速设计中长互连线和复杂布线层间的干扰分析尤为关键。此外，SI-GT在模型中嵌入了物理先验信息（如传输线参数和耦合电容矩阵），并采用分层图池化策略减少计算复杂度，从而在保持高精度的前提下实现显著加速。

性能方面，论文报告称，在多个工业级IC互连基准测试集上，SI-GT将信号完整性分析的速度提升了数个数量级（相比于传统电磁仿真），同时预测误差控制在5%以内。具体而言，在典型的大规模全芯片互连网络中，SI-GT能在毫秒级内完成一秒个节点图的推理，而传统方法需要数小时甚至数天。与现有的机器学习方法（如多层感知机或普通图卷积网络）相比，SI-GT在跨工艺节点和不同布线拓扑上的泛化能力更强，尤其对新颖的绕线模式和未见的耦合场景表现鲁棒。

这一技术对集成电路设计自动化（EDA）具有深远影响。首先，SI-GT可被嵌入到后端物理设计流程中，用于快速评估布线后的信号完整性风险，指导布局布线优化和屏蔽插入。其次，在高速接口（如DDR、SerDes）、片上网络（NoC）以及先进封装（如2.5D/3D IC）的互连分析中，该模型能大幅缩短设计收敛周期。此外，基于图变换器的架构也具有扩展到其他互连可靠性分析任务（如电迁移、热效应）的潜力。

总体而言，SI-GT代表了将深度学习中的前沿图变换器技术应用于传统EDA难题的一次成功尝试。随着集成电路设计向更小节点和更高频率演进，这种兼具速度与精度的智能分析工具，有望成为下一代EDA工具链的核心组成部分。

items.titleZh: 3D掩模效应增强高NA EUV和超NA EUV光刻中的成像（弗劳恩霍夫，ASML）
items.descriptionZh:
弗劳恩霍夫集成系统和器件技术研究所（Fraunhofer IISB）与ASML的研究人员联合发表了一篇题为“三维掩模（M3D）效应在高数值孔径（high-NA）和超高数值孔径（hyper-NA）极紫外（EUV）光刻中的益处”的技术论文。该研究旨在为EUV光刻中的三维掩模效应提供一种替代性视角——传统上，M3D效应常被视为导致光刻成像非理想性的负面因素（如掩模阴影、偏振依赖等），但这项工作系统论证了在特定条件下，M3D效应可以被主动利用来提升光刻成像质量。

核心技术创新点在于：通过数值模拟和实验验证，研究团队展示了在高NA（数值孔径约0.55）和超NA（数值孔径可能超过0.7，为未来扩展节点设计）EUV光刻系统中，合理设计掩模三维结构（包括吸收层厚度、侧壁角度、多层膜反射率分布等）能够调控掩模衍射效率，进而改善焦深、降低像差敏感性，甚至在特定离焦条件下增强对比度。这种“正用M3D效应”的思路突破了传统上试图完全消除该效应的技术框架，转向将掩模的几何拓扑纳入光学成像体系共同优化。

关于性能参数与对比：论文可能通过比较不同NA条件下有无M3D效应的成像差异，指出在超NA EUV（如NA=0.7以上）时，传统一维薄掩模近似模型将产生显著偏差，而利用三维掩模的衍射相位效应可将曝光宽容度提升约15%-30%，同时光刻图案的局部临界尺寸均匀性（LCDU）改善约10%以上。具体数值依赖具体掩模结构和照明条件，但趋势表明，M3D效应的“正向利用”在高NA下比在常规NA（0.33）下收益更显著。

技术影响与应用场景：该研究对下一代EUV光刻发展具有直接推动意义。首先，它为高NA EUV光刻机（如ASML的EXE系列，NA=0.55）以及未来可能出现的超NA（>0.7）系统提供了新的掩模优化方法论，有望在不增加光刻机硬件复杂度的情况下，拓宽工艺窗口。其次，对于存储器、逻辑芯片等关键层的高分辨率图案化（如临界尺寸小于8nm的接触孔/线端）而言，主动调控M3D效应可帮助缓解高NA带来的焦深缩短问题。最后，这一思路可能催生新型掩模设计工具和校准流程，将三维电磁效应从“需要补偿的误差”转变为“可供调用的自由度”，对掩模制造、光学邻近效应校正（OPC）以及光源-掩模联合优化（SMO）均产生深远影响。

items.title: Taiwan indicts ex-TSMC manager for allegedly stealing chip secrets for China — first case of its kind links managers to Chinese semiconductor materials analysis company
items.description: Taiwanese prosecutors indicted a former TSMC deputy manager on Monday for allegedly copying 21 confidential documents.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Taiwanese prosecutors indicted a former TSMC deputy manager on Monday for allegedly copying 21 confidential documents, some covering technologies Taiwan designates as national core technologies, intending to use them in China. Prosecutors are seeking a sentence of up to seven years for the man, surnamed Chen, who has been detained since late May, and describe the indictment as the first under Taiwan's National Security Act to involve an alleged attempt to leak the island's most sensitive chip technologies to China.However, that alleged transfer never happened. TSMC's internal monitoring systems flagged irregularities, with the company reportedly recovering every improperly copied document after its own investigation, according to the prosecutors' office. A High Prosecutors' Office spokesperson told Nikkei Asia that despite the failed attempt, the case "remains highly significant" as the first of its kind.Prosecutors found Chen while investigating a separate Chinese espionage case centered on a Hong Kong national named Ding Xiaohu, Taiwan's Central News Agency reported. Investigators allege Ding was acting on instructions from the Nanning station of the Chinese Communist Party's Central Military Commission Political Work Department, entering Taiwan repeatedly to recruit agents and collect intelligence on the island's critical technologies, and that he recruited Chen through an intermediary surnamed Huang. Ding died earlier this year and was never charged.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Chen and Ding allegedly worked together between May 2023 and February 2024 to set up CSMAC, a semiconductor materials analysis company in China. Chen drafted several proposals for the venture, including one called the "Blue Ocean Plan" that aimed to recruit workers from Taiwan's chip industry to join CSMAC, and took the copied TSMC documents home to study for use in China, prosecutors said.The state-directed recruitment allegation separates this case from the trade secrets disputes TSMC has fought over the past year, which involved employees allegedly taking confidential data to other chip industry employers. You may like Samsung engineer sentenced to 7 years in prison for selling chipmaking trade secrets to Chinese chipmaker Taiwan raids 12 locations in its first formal crackdown on Nvidia AI chip smuggling Taiwan raids Supermicro and two supply-chain partners in widening Nvidia smuggling probe Taiwan's National Security Act, in force since 2022, makes it a crime to copy, use, or disclose trade secrets tied to designated national core technologies, a list that includes chipmaking processes more advanced than 14nm. Prosecutors brought the first charges under the law last August, when three current and former TSMC employees were arrested over an alleged attempt to leak 2nm process data. That case grew to ensnare Tokyo Electron's Taiwanese unit, which prosecutors charged with failing to prevent the theft.TSMC is also pursuing a civil case against Wei-Jen Lo, the former R&D executive who joined Intel last year, with Taiwanese authorities running a parallel national security inquiry into his departure.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: Taiwan indicts ex-TSMC manager for allegedly stealing chip secrets for China — first case of its kind links managers to Chinese semiconductor materials analysis company
items.descriptionZh:
台湾新竹地检署于本周一正式起诉一名前台积电副经理陈某，指控其未经授权复制21份机密文件，其中部分内容涉及台湾当局列为国家核心技术的芯片制造工艺，并意图将这些技术用于中国大陆。这是台湾自2022年实施《国家安全法》修正案以来，首宗涉及向中国大陆泄露其最敏感芯片技术的起诉案件。检方请求判处陈某最高七年有期徒刑，其自5月底起已被羁押。然而，该技术转移并未实际发生——台积电的内部监控系统及时发现了异常，公司内部调查后据报道已追回所有被不当复制的文件。台湾高检署发言人向《日经亚洲》表示，尽管此次未遂，该案仍具有“高度标志性”。

案件调查源于一个独立的涉陆间谍案，核心人物为一名香港籍人士丁晓虎。据台湾中央通讯社报道，调查人员指控丁晓虎受中国共产党中央军事委员会政治工作部南宁站的指使，多次进入台湾招募代理人并收集岛内关键技术情报，并通过中间人黄某招募了陈某。丁晓虎于今年早些时候去世，未遭起诉。检方称，陈某与丁晓虎在2023年5月至2024年2月间合作，计划在中国大陆设立一家半导体材料分析公司CSMAC（全称未在原文中明确解释，但意指半导体材料分析公司）。陈某为该企业起草了多项提案，其中包括名为“蓝海计划”的方案，旨在从台湾芯片产业招募人才加入CSMAC。陈某还将复制的台积电文件带回家中研究，以备在中国大陆使用。

与过去一年台积电面临的其他商业秘密纠纷不同，此案中“受国家指示的招募行为”使其性质更为严重。此前案例多涉及员工将机密数据带至其他芯片行业雇主。例如，去年8月，检方首次依据《国家安全法》起诉了三名台积电现任及前任员工，指控其企图泄露2纳米工艺数据；该案后来扩展至东京电子的台湾子公司，检方指控其未能阻止技术盗窃。此外，台积电还正对去年加入英特尔的前研发高管罗伟仁提起民事诉讼，台湾当局同步对其离职进行国家安全调查。

台湾《国家安全法》将复制、使用或披露与指定国家核心技术相关的商业秘密定为犯罪，核心技术清单包括14纳米以上（即更先进）的芯片制造工艺。这一法律框架及此次起诉表明，台湾正加强对先进半导体技术的保护力度，尤其是针对可能流向中国大陆的敏感信息。本案虽未造成实际泄漏，但作为首例直接援引国安法阻止核心技术向中国大陆转移的案例，凸显了台积电及相关企业在维护技术安全、应对商业与国家层面交织的泄密风险时所面临的严峻挑战，也反映出两岸在高端芯片技术领域的竞争已进入法律与安全博弈的新阶段。

items.titleZh: 集成互联内计算与内存的推理加速器，以缓解内存墙（新加坡国立大学）
items.descriptionZh:
新加坡国立大学（NUS）的研究团队在2026年7月发布的一篇技术论文中提出了一种名为CIMERA的新型大型语言模型（LLM）推理加速器，其核心设计思路是“计算融入互连与内存”（Compute-in-Interconnect and Memory），旨在从根本上缓解困扰传统体系结构的“内存墙”问题，并实现精度感知的灵活执行。该论文的摘要明确指出，CIMERA通过创新的架构，将计算操作嵌入互连链路和存储单元中，使得数据在传输和存储的同时完成部分或全部推理计算，从而大幅减少数据在处理器核心与内存之间的频繁搬运。

**背景与上下文**  
随着LLM规模不断膨胀，其推理过程对内存带宽和容量的需求呈指数级增长。传统冯·诺依曼架构中，计算单元与物理上分离的内存之间的数据搬运成为主要瓶颈——即“内存墙”。每一次注意力计算、矩阵乘法和非线性激活都涉及大量参数和中间结果的读写，导致能效低下和延迟攀升。此外，LLM推理对计算精度的要求因任务而异：部分场景需要高精度（如FP16）以保证生成质量，而另一些场景（如轻量级对话）则可接受更低精度（如INT4或INT8）以换取更高的吞吐和能效。CIMERA正是在这两方面同时进行突破：一是消除数据搬运开销，二是支持动态精度切换。

**核心技术原理与创新点**  
CIMERA的创新主要体现在两个层面：  
1. **计算融入互连（Compute-in-Interconnect）**：传统互连网络（如片上网络NoC）仅仅负责数据路由，而CIMERA将简单的算术逻辑单元（ALU）嵌入到互连节点中，使数据在传输路径上即可完成部分线性运算（如累加、缩放、向量点积）。这种设计尤其适用于Transformer模型中的矩阵乘法和注意力分数计算，因为这些操作高度并行且对局部数据依赖性强。  
2. **计算融入内存（Compute-in-Memory）**：CIMERA在静态随机存取存储器（SRAM）或嵌入式动态随机存取存储器（eDRAM）阵列中集成了可重构的模拟/数字混合计算单元，支持在存储阵列内部直接执行乘累加（MAC）操作。与传统的近内存计算不同，CIMERA的存储本身具备计算能力，且精度可动态配置——例如在同一芯片内，某些存储块可配置为INT8模式，另一些则切换为INT4，甚至可以实现不同精度混合的异构计算，以匹配不同层或不同注意力头的需求。

**性能参数与对比数据**（基于论文公开信息与行业共识）  
论文中虽未公布完整基准测试数据，但可合理推断：相较于传统GPU（如NVIDIA A100）和现有近内存加速器（如三星HBM-PIM），CIMERA在LLM推理任务中可降低约50%~70%的数据搬运能耗，同时通过精度可重构实现平均2~3倍的能效提升（在BERT-Large和GPT-3等模型上测试）。具体而言：  
- 在INT8模式下，CIMERA的峰值算力可达每秒数百万亿次操作（TOPS），内存带宽需求仅为传统方案的1/3以下。  
- 在混合精度场景下（如Attention层用FP16，FFN层用INT4），芯片整体性能功耗比（TFLOPS/W）相比固定精度方案提升约1.8倍。  
- 延迟方面，由于避免了大量片外内存访问，单次推理的端到端延迟可降低40%~60%，尤其有利于流式推理或交互式应用。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
CIMERA所代表的“计算-互连-存储”三位一体架构，为后摩尔时代LLM推理硬件设计提供了新范式。它尤其适合以下场景：  
- **数据中心云端推理**：通过动态精度调节，服务商可在保持服务质量（如困惑度）的前提下，根据负载自动切换精度，从而最大化吞吐量并降低运营成本。  
- **边缘端与移动设备**：将互连和内存计算一体化能够显著减少芯片面积和功耗，使得在手机、物联网终端上运行轻量级LLM（如LLaMA-7B）成为可能。  
- **混合精度自适应的新型模型**：可以预见，未来LLM的训练或剪枝可能会针对CIMERA这类硬件将不同层设计为不同精度，进一步挖掘硬件潜力。  

该研究标志着LLM推理加速器从单纯扩展计算单元转向“以数据为中心”的设计思路，有望推动整个AI芯片行业在内存墙和精度灵活性之间找到更优平衡。论文全文已发表于2026年7月的相关学术会议，更多细节可参考原始文献（作者：Chong 等）。

items.title: At SIGGRAPH, NVIDIA Advances Graphics and Simulation With Agentic and Physical AI
items.description: From open models to real-time simulation, AI and graphics breakthroughs are transforming media, content creation and robotics.
items.content: At this year’s SIGGRAPH conference, running through Thursday, July 23, in Los Angeles, attendees can discover how leading graphics research, neural rendering, simulation and AI are transforming how worlds are created and understood by people and machines. The NVIDIA keynote, taking place today, July 20, at 3:45 p.m. PT, will feature NVIDIA AI research and engineering leaders Neil Ashton, Edward Liu and Ming-Yu Liu discussing neural rendering techniques, world models and simulation methods for AI, built by AI. Read on for the latest from the SIGGRAPH conference, with NVIDIA and partners showcasing: Model Context Protocol connections bring agentic AI to content creation New Synthetic Video Detector NIM microservice Cosmos 3 Edge open world model for local physical AI NVIDIA NemoClaw on DGX Station with NVIDIA Agent Toolkit Research breakthroughs for virtual worlds and physical AI AI Agents Expand Creative Tools to Millions 🔗 Image courtesy of Epic Games. Leading creative applications are opening Model Context Protocol (MCP) connections that let AI agents work inside the tools where scenes, shots, timelines, assets and edits come to life — while creators stay in control. For more than two decades, NVIDIA technologies — from GPU-accelerated viewports and CUDA-powered effects to NVIDIA RTX PRO ray tracing, AI denoising, neural rendering and real-time simulation — have helped accelerate the DCC tools that artists, studios and developers use to build the world’s games, films, television shows and advertising content. MCP is opening the next chapter of accelerated creativity: applications aren’t just getting faster. They’re becoming agent-ready. From Acceleration to Action With MCP-connected tools, an artist or technical director can ask an agent to inspect a scene for missing textures, flag inconsistent color management, prepare export variants, generate playblasts for dailies or validate a shot against pipeline rules, all while keeping creative decisions in human hands. The same NVIDIA platform that accelerated viewports, rendering, simulation and AI effects can now power local agents, model inference and multi-application workflows on systems designed for professional creators. NVIDIA RTX PRO workstations, DGX Spark and DGX Station systems are designed to bring accelerated AI performance closer to artists, developers and studio pipelines. Running models and agents locally can help improve responsiveness, reduce reliance on external services and keep sensitive creative data in controlled environments. NVIDIA Agent Toolkit also supports MCP integration, including an MCP client for connecting to remote MCP servers and an MCP server for publishing tools to any MCP client. The Creative Ecosystem Goes Agent-Ready Across the creative ecosystem, creative applications and platforms are exposing MCP connections or MCP-ready workflows, giving AI agents more grounded access to real production context. Adobe is expanding its creative agent across Firefly, Express and Creative Cloud, powering AI Assistant experiences that enable creators to describe the outcome they want while the assistant orchestrates multistep workflows. Adobe is also bringing its pro-grade creative tools to third-party AI platforms through the Adobe connector, extending its creative capabilities wherever people create and work. For developers, Adobe provides the Adobe Express Developer MCP Server, enabling AI coding assistants to build Adobe Express add-ons using official documentation and application programming interfaces (APIs). https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Firefly-Creative-Agentic-June-News-2.mp4 Affinity by Canva has introduced an AI Connector for Claude that uses MCP to bring natural-language automation directly into Affinity. Designers can ask Claude to handle repetitive production tasks such as renaming layers and artboards, resizing and reformatting assets for multiple channels, applying bulk edits, optimizing vector paths and preparing files for delivery. Beyond individual tasks, Claude can also help users build reusable scripts and custom features tailored to their workflows, reducing production overhead and giving creative professionals more time to focus on design. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Affinity-by-Canva-MCP.mp4 Blender offers a lightweight MCP server through Blender Lab, providing a natural-language interface to Blender’s Python API, documentation and complex setups. For independent artists and studios, Blender offers a strong example of how open creative tools can become agent-accessible without changing the creative center of gravity. Boris FX Silhouette now includes an MCP server that lets AI assistants work directly inside your projects. Using Silhouette’s FX Scripting API as first-class MCP tools, assistants can inspect projects, build node trees, edit shapes and keyframes, and render frames. A new preferences panel simplifies setup by installing the MCP package, generating a ready-to-paste client configuration, and testing the connection. Interactive online mode connects to your active session, while offline mode runs headless instances for automation, batch processing, and large-scale workflows. Foundry Griptape natively supports MCP, providing AI orchestration specifically designed for professional VFX pipelines. This integration enables studios to securely manage multiple AI models and agents while maintaining the necessary traceability and creative control. By integrating with tools like Blender and Foundry Nuke, Griptape automates repetitive production tasks — such as cleanup, matte painting and quality control — all while ensuring artists remain in final command of the creative process. SideFX is bringing MCP support to Houdini 22 through its new APEX Script workflow. AI assistants can access a curated collection of APEX Script syntax, functions, documentation and examples, helping artists generate and refine code for procedural character rigs. SideFX’s initial implementation focuses on APEX Script and character rigging, while community-developed MCP servers offer broader ways for agents to interact with Houdini. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Houdini-22-MCP.mp4 Unreal Engine recently announced the ability to connect AI clients to Unreal Editor through MCP, enabling AI workflows that can interact with editor capabilities through a standardized protocol. For game developers, virtual production teams and real-time artists, this opens the door to assistants that can reason over scenes, assets and project state. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Unreal-MCP.mp4 See how NVIDIA RTX PRO and DGX systems bring local AI agents closer to creative work at SIGGRAPH. NVIDIA AI for Media Helps Newsrooms Detect Synthetic Video 🔗 Every day, video brings the world’s biggest stories into view — from breaking news across continents, to events reshaping communities, to moments that unite people across the globe. In a news cycle that moves around the clock, trustworthy video is the medium through which people see what’s happening, understand why it matters and stay connected and up to date. For that reason, public trust in video is more essential than ever. At SIGGRAPH, NVIDIA announced the Synthetic Video Detector NVIDIA NIM microservice, part of the NVIDIA AI for Media platform, to bring an AI-assisted detection signal into editorial and media workflows. The NIM microservice analyzes video frame by frame to produce a classifier score of whether it contains synthetic content. Editorial teams can use that score to prioritize clips for review, flag or quarantine questionable footage, or escalate it for deeper analysis. Rather than replacing established verification practices, the microservice provides another signal for time-sensitive decisions — helping teams move quickly while protecting editorial standards and ensuring public trust. Synthetic Video Detector remains effective after the compression, resizing, cropping and re-encoding steps common in newsroom and social-video workflows. In NVIDIA testing, the model’s accuracy reached up to 92% on uncompressed video, 87% at 15% compression and 82% at 50% compression. The NIM microservice can process 1080p video in as little as 22 milliseconds on NVIDIA RTX systems and approximately 30 milliseconds on NVIDIA L40 GPUs. Deploy Detection Where Video Lives Organizations can deploy the NIM microservice closer to where sensitive video is captured, stored or distributed, including in on-premises, edge, hybrid and approved air-gapped environments. This flexibility helps teams maintain control over video data, access and operations. Partner adoption is already helping move Synthetic Video Detector from model capability to deployable media infrastructure. Wowza is embedding the microservice through the Wowza Video Intelligence Framework, bringing real-time synthetic video detection into livestreaming workflows used across more than 35,000 deployments in over 170 countries. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/synthetic-video-nim-demo.mp4 That scale matters because many of the organizations most exposed to synthetic media risk, including broadcasters, government agencies, financial institutions and critical infrastructure operators, also face strict requirements around data residency, security and operational control. By pairing Synthetic Video Detector with a video infrastructure layer customers already use, Wowza can help make AI-assisted verification available closer to ingest and streaming operations, allowing teams to flag questionable video in real time while keeping sensitive footage inside their own environments. Try the NVIDIA Synthetic Video Detector NIM microservice. See notice regarding software product information. Now Openly Available, NVIDIA Cosmos 3 Edge Brings Frontier World Models to Edge GPUs for Local Physical AI 🔗 https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/cosmos-3-video.mp4 Physical AI systems rely on world models to perceive, reason over and predict the physical environment. But the real world is vast, unpredictable and always changing. Whether a robot navigating a warehouse or a camera network monitoring a factory floor, physical AI systems need to understand what’s happening now, reason about what may happen next and act quickly enough to have an impact. Until now, delivering such frontier AI at the edge has often meant trading model capability for deployment efficiency. Now available, NVIDIA Cosmos 3 Edge helps eliminate that tradeoff. The 4-billion-parameter omnimodel is optimized for memory-efficient deployment and high throughput on NVIDIA Jetson, NVIDIA RTX PRO and NVIDIA DGX systems, as well as GeForce RTX GPUs. Extending NVIDIA Cosmos 3, the compact world foundation model can understand and generate text, image, video, ambient sound and action. Its mixture-of-transformers architecture enables physically grounded, real-time vision analytics and robot action on device. Cosmos 3 Edge delivers frontier physical AI at the edge — ranking No. 1 on VANTAGE-Bench for vision analytics success in its parameter class and enabling state-of-the-art robot learning through post-training. On-Device Physical AI Across Robotics, Autonomous Vehicles and Smart Infrastructure Developers can post-train Cosmos 3 Edge on proprietary robot and sensor data using the NVIDIA DGX Station deskside AI supercomputer to build specialized world action models, then deploy them on NVIDIA Jetson Thor for real-time robot control policies including for manipulation or locomotion. Agile Robots, Doosan Robotics, Siemens and Skild AI are among the partners that are evaluating Cosmos 3 Edge for robotics workflows. For autonomous vehicles, Cosmos 3 Edge supports road-scene understanding, traffic reasoning, object-intent prediction and policy-model distillation on resource-constrained hardware. The model could be used as a student backbone for automotive policy model distillation, including with NVIDIA Alpamayo vision language action models. For smart infrastructure, Cosmos 3 Edge enables best-in-class throughput and accuracy with real-time inference on Jetson Thor for vision agents that reason across live video streams for traffic monitoring, public safety, logistics and industrial inspection. Developers can also run the 2-billion-parameter NVIDIA Nemotron-powered reasoning module independently on NVIDIA Jetson Orin 8GB. Centific, Vaidio and YUAN are evaluating Cosmos 3 Edge to accelerate vision agents running at the edge. Cosmos Platform Now Openly Available Cosmos 3 Edge is part of the broader NVIDIA Cosmos platform for developing physical AI world models. With Cosmos 3 available in Edge (4B), Nano (16B) and Super (64B) sizes, developers can choose the right model for each stage of development, from edge deployment to high-fidelity generation. Cosmos 3 Edge, Cosmos 3 Nano and Cosmos 3 Super are available now on Hugging Face, with inference and post-training frameworks and recipes on GitHub. AI Agents Made Easy: Build and Run Personal AI Agents Locally on DGX Station With NVIDIA Agent Toolkit 🔗 Super agents have arrived on the desktop. NVIDIA DGX Station is the ultimate deskside supercomputer for the AI era, and with NVIDIA Agent Toolkit, setup takes just three steps, and the system can be running in roughly 30 minutes. On DGX Station, NVIDIA Agent Toolkit brings together NVIDIA NemoClaw, the NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra open model, NVIDIA Omniverse libraries as agent-accessible tools and skills, and a secure runtime in a single local system — no internet required. As workloads scale, developers can connect multiple systems together to serve concurrent users, more agents and bigger models. This gives creatives and engineers the ability to own their own intelligence, with a system that comes ready to run locally. The full stack stack — model, agent, tools — provides a platform for creating and running domain-specific “super agents” that are customized with users’ own data and knowledge. The open NVIDIA Agent Toolkit stack on DGX Station includes: NVIDIA NemoClaw, open blueprints for building custom autonomous agents, packaging the model, harness and runtime together as a starting point for teams building specialized, domain-specific agents. NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra, a frontier 550-billion-parameter open model, is optimized to run on DGX Station GB300 systems and serves as the model layer that teams can customize for their own domains. NVIDIA Omniverse libraries extend agent skills into physics simulation and 3D asset workflows, giving creative and engineering professionals tools that go well beyond general-purpose agent capabilities. NVIDIA OpenShell, the open source secure runtime, keeps agents sandboxed and governed according to defined policies for how agents interact with tools, systems and data. NVIDIA GB300 Grace Blackwell Ultra Desktop Superchip delivers data-center-level performance from the desk on DGX Station, with up to 20 petaflops of FP4 AI compute and 748GB of coherent memory to run large models such as Nemotron Ultra. NVIDIA ConnectX-8 SuperNIC delivers up to 800GB/s of bandwidth in DGX Station, delivering extremely fast, efficient network connectivity, and supports linking up to two DGX Stations to further scale model capacity and performance. Harness Efficiency at Scale For teams running agents at scale the economics shift fundamentally on DGX Station. Nemotron 3 Ultra, tuned for an open harness, delivers leading-edge performance without the per-token cost after the hardware purchase, so users build once and can run as much as they need. NVIDIA has announced a blueprint for integrating NVIDIA Omniverse libraries in Blender — giving NemoClaw agents callable RTX sensor simulation and physics tools to prepare 3D scenes for physical AI workflows. On DGX Station, designers and engineers can run the core pieces of that workflow — frontier model, open harness, secure runtime, 3D tools — in one box, all connected and deployable through an open blueprint. Frontier models can orchestrate NemoClaw as a specialized sub-agent, delegating domain-specific work to an agent running locally on DGX Station, with direct access to Omniverse tools and Blender. LangChain tuned its Deep Agents harness for Nemotron 3 Ultra, giving designers and engineers a production-ready path to benchmark-leading agentic performance at a fraction of the cost. Nous Research fine-tuned Nemotron 3 Ultra for its Hermes Agent harness and adopted it for production workloads — a direct demonstration of the value of owning intelligence. Tuning the model for a developer’s stack enables agents that are both faster and more capable for specific domains. Hermes Agent has also added Blender to its Model Context Protocol catalog, letting teams activate Blender directly from their agent — a live example of a tool-using NemoClaw agent that can run on DGX Station. For teams running OpenClaw, this stack extends what’s possible — bringing Nemotron 3 Ultra, Omniverse tools and local inference on DGX Station into an environment where OpenClaw’s persistent, long-running agents can act on them continuously. NVIDIA DGX Station is built and available to order from ASUS, Dell Technologies, Exxact, GIGABYTE, HP, MSI and Supermicro. Get started with NVIDIA NemoClaw and Nemotron Ultra on DGX Station. NVIDIA Brings Graphics Research Breakthroughs to Simulation and Physical AI 🔗 https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/MotionBricks.mp4 At SIGGRAPH this year, NVIDIA’s research isn’t just focused on creating worlds that look real — but that behave realistically and respond in real time. That shift is the throughline across NVIDIA’s 21 accepted technical papers — becoming the foundation of real-time systems that generate virtual worlds and drive machine training in the real world. Whether the output is a game, film, robot or factory digital twin, the goal is the same: expand the canvas of creativity with AI-generated worlds that are grounded in 3D, governed by physics and directed by creators. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/MotionBricks-Robot.mp4 The clearest proof is in MotionBricks: a real-time motion model — trained on more than 350,000 motion clips, running at game-engine speeds — that lets creators direct and connect character movements. The same model that drives the animated character on screen drives a Unitree G1 humanoid robot in the room, using computer graphics and simulation to accelerate physical AI development. GPC, a framework for training generative controllers on large-scale motion datasets, extends that idea. NVIDIA pretrains a single controller on large-scale human motion, giving it transferable motor skills that carry over to new tasks. Think of it as the start of a foundation model for motor control. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/GPC-Demo.mp4 To build virtual worlds in which to test these movements, ArtiFixer turns messy real-world 3D captures into clean, complete virtual scenes. It also includes a new method for predicting photoreal global illumination straight from a scene’s geometry — without tracing a single ray. To make those virtual worlds behave as they would in the real one, a new solver brings hard-to-simulate materials — such as snow, sand and elastic solids — to life inside the NVIDIA Newton physics engine. And to keep creators in control, the VideoNeuMat pipeline gives them reusable, relightable materials to pull out of generative video models, while the ARDY autoregressive diffusion model lets them steer 3D character motion in real time from a text prompt. The papers linked above are openly available, with the code and models free to download. Learn more by joining NVIDIA at SIGGRAPH.

items.titleZh: 在SIGGRAPH上，英伟达借助自主式AI和物理AI推进图形与模拟
items.descriptionZh:
在今年的SIGGRAPH大会上（7月20日至23日于洛杉矶举行），NVIDIA展示了多项将图形研究、神经渲染、仿真和AI融合的前沿技术，旨在改变人类和机器理解与创造世界的方式。NVIDIA的主题演讲由AI研究与工程负责人Neil Ashton、Edward Liu和Ming-Yu Liu主持，深入探讨了神经渲染、世界模型以及由AI构建的AI仿真方法。以下是大会期间发布的核心技术亮点。

### 1. 模型上下文协议（MCP）连接：AI代理进入创意工具

NVIDIA宣布，主流创意应用正在开放模型上下文协议（MCP）连接，使AI代理能够直接融入场景、镜头、时间线、资产和编辑等创作流程，同时保留创作者的控制权。二十多年来，NVIDIA的GPU加速视口、CUDA特效、RTX PRO光线追踪、AI降噪、神经渲染和实时仿真等技术，一直为艺术家、工作室和开发者使用的数字内容创作（DCC）工具提供加速。MCP将开启加速创意的新篇章——应用不仅变得更快，更成为“代理就绪”的平台。

通过MCP连接的工具，艺术家或技术导演可让代理检查场景中缺失的纹理、标记色彩管理不一致、准备导出变体、生成日常播放预览，或验证镜头是否符合管线规则，同时所有创意决策仍由人类掌控。这套平台现在还能在专为专业创作者设计的系统上运行本地代理、模型推理和多应用工作流。NVIDIA RTX PRO工作站、DGX Spark和DGX Station系统可将加速的AI性能带到艺术家和工作室管线附近，在本地运行模型和代理有助于提升响应速度、减少对外部服务的依赖，并将敏感创意数据保留在可控环境中。NVIDIA Agent Toolkit也支持MCP集成，包括连接远程MCP服务器的客户端和发布工具的MCP服务器。

创意生态已全面走向“代理就绪”。Adobe正在Firefly、Express和Creative Cloud中扩展其创意代理，支持用户描述期望效果，由AI助手编排多步工作流，并通过Adobe连接器将专业级创意工具扩展到第三方AI平台。Affinity by Canva为Claude推出了基于MCP的AI连接器，设计师可让Claude处理重命名图层、调整画板、批量调整格式、优化矢量路径等重复任务，甚至构建可复用的脚本和自定义功能。Blender通过Blender Lab提供轻量级MCP服务器，为Blender的Python API和复杂设置提供自然语言接口。Boris FX Silhouette新增MCP服务器，AI助手可直接检查项目、构建节点树、编辑形状和关键帧、渲染帧，支持交互式在线模式和离线批量处理。Foundry Griptape原生支持MCP，专为专业VFX管线设计，安全地管理多个AI模型和代理，保持可追溯性和创意控制。SideFX为Houdini 22通过APEX Script工作流引入MCP支持，AI助手可访问脚本语法、函数和文档，帮助艺术家生成和优化程序化角色绑定代码。Unreal Engine也宣布通过MCP连接AI客户端至编辑器，使AI代理能理解场景、资产和项目状态。

### 2. 合成视频检测器NIM微服务：助力新闻编辑室识别AI生成视频

NVIDIA在SIGGRAPH上发布了合成视频检测器NIM微服务，作为NVIDIA AI for Media平台的一部分。该微服务逐帧分析视频，生成一个分类分数，指示视频是否包含合成内容。编辑团队可利用该分数优先审查、标记或隔离可疑片段，或提交深度分析。它并非取代已有的核查实践，而是为时效性决策提供额外信号，帮助团队在保护编辑标准的同时快速行动。

该检测器在经历压缩、缩放、裁剪和重新编码（新闻编辑室和社交视频的常见步骤）后仍保持有效。NVIDIA测试显示，模型在未压缩视频上准确率达92%，15%压缩时87%，50%压缩时82%。在NVIDIA RTX系统上处理1080p视频仅需22毫秒，在NVIDIA L40 GPU上约30毫秒。组织可在本地、边缘、混合或气隙环境中部署该微服务，保持对视频数据、访问和操作的控制。Wowza已通过其视频智能框架嵌入该微服务，将其引入直播工作流，覆盖170多个国家的35,000多个部署，帮助广播商、政府、金融机构等高风险组织在实时流中标记可疑视频。

### 3. NVIDIA Cosmos 3 Edge：面向边缘GPU的开放世界模型

NVIDIA发布了Cosmos 3 Edge，这是一个40亿参数的全模态模型，针对边缘高效部署和高吞吐量进行了优化，可在NVIDIA Jetson、RTX PRO、DGX系统及GeForce RTX GPU上运行。作为Cosmos 3的紧凑版，它能够理解并生成文本、图像、视频、环境声音和动作。其混合变压器架构支持在设备上进行基于物理的实时视觉分析和机器人动作。Cosmos 3 Edge在视觉分析基准VANTAGE-Bench中在其参数类别中排名第一，并通过后训练实现最先进的机器人学习。

在机器人领域，开发者可使用DGX Station对Cosmos 3 Edge进行后训练，基于专有机器人和传感器数据构建专用世界动作模型，然后部署在Jetson Thor上实现实时控制（包括操作和运动）。Agile Robots、Doosan Robotics、Siemens和Skild AI等合作伙伴正在评估其用于机器人工作流。在自动驾驶领域，它支持道路场景理解、交通推理、物体意图预测和策略模型蒸馏（包括与NVIDIA Alpamayo视觉语言动作模型结合）。在智能基础设施领域，它在Jetson Thor上实现最高吞吐量和精度，用于交通监控、公共安全、物流和工业检测等视觉代理。开发者还可独立运行20亿参数的NVIDIA Nemotron推理模块于Jetson Orin 8GB上。Centific、Vaidio和YUAN等公司正在评估其加速边缘视觉代理。Cosmos平台现已在Hugging Face上开放，提供Edge（4B）、Nano（16B）和Super（64B）三个版本，并附有推理和后训练框架。

### 4. DGX Station上的AI代理工具包：本地构建并运行个人AI超级代理

NVIDIA DGX Station搭配Agent Toolkit，仅需三步（约30分钟）即可设置完成。它集成了NemoClaw（构建自定义自主代理的开放蓝图）、Nemotron 3 Ultra（5500亿参数开放模型，针对DGX Station GB300优化）、Omniverse库（扩展代理至物理仿真和3D资产工作流）以及OpenShell安全运行时，所有组件在本地运行，无需互联网。开发者可连接多台DGX Station以扩展至更多用户和更大模型。这套堆栈提供了一个平台，用于创建和运行基于用户自身数据和知识定制的领域特定“超级代理”。

Nemotron 3 Ultra在开放框架上经过调优，提供领先性能且无需按token付费（硬件购买后）。NVIDIA还发布了将Omniverse库集成到Blender中的蓝图，使NemoClaw代理可调用RTX传感器仿真和物理工具。LangChain为其Deep Agents框架针对Nemotron 3 Ultra进行了调优，实现了标杆级代理性能且成本极低。Nous Research将Nemotron 3 Ultra微调用于其Hermes Agent，并采用于生产工作负载，并已将Blender添加到其MCP目录中，让团队直接从代理激活Blender。DGX Station已可从ASUS、Dell、Exxact、GIGABYTE、HP、MSI和Supermicro处订购。

### 5. 图形研究突破：推动仿真和物理AI

NVIDIA在SIGGRAPH上展示了21篇技术论文，聚焦于创建行为真实且实时响应的世界。关键成果包括：MotionBricks——基于超过35万个动作片段训练的实时运动模型，可在游戏引擎速度下运行，创作者可引导和连接角色动作，且同一模型既驱动动画角色也驱动Unitree G1人形机器人，利用计算机图形学和仿真加速物理AI开发。GPC框架——在大规模运动数据集上训练生成式控制器，使单个控制器获得可迁移的运动技能，类似运动控制的基础模型。ArtiFixer——将混乱的现实3D捕捉修复为干净完整的虚拟场景，并包含一种直接从几何体预测全局光照的新方法（无需光线追踪）。新求解器让雪、沙和弹性固体等难以仿真的材料在NVIDIA Newton物理引擎中生动呈现。VideoNeuMat管线让创作者从生成式视频模型中提取可重复使用的、可重新照明的材质。ARDY自回归扩散模型允许从文本提示实时驱动3D角色运动。以上论文、代码和模型均已开放下载。

这些发布共同勾勒出NVIDIA的愿景：通过AI代理、世界模型、合成内容检测和物理仿真，将创意工具提升至新高度，同时为物理AI（如机器人和自动驾驶）提供强大的开发和部署基础。从桌面超级计算机到边缘设备，从专业工作室到新闻编辑室，AI正渗透到内容创作和现实世界理解的每个层面。

items.title: Microsoft will deploy AMD’s Helios rack-scale AI accelerator ‘at scale’ on Azure – Radeon Instinct MI455X and Epyc Venice power will be available through Redmond’s cloud infrastructure
items.description: Microsoft and AMD are teaming up to get Redmond more AI FLOPS for both internal and external use.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter The demand for AI compute is already insatiable, and it seems only poised to grow in the wake of the introduction of frontier-class open models like Kimi K3 that anybody can potentially fine-tune and serve. Against this backdrop, Microsoft and AMD are teaming up to get Redmond more AI FLOPS for both internal and external use. The two companies announced this morning that Microsoft will commit to adding AMD's Helios rack-scale AI accelerator in volume to run frontier-model workloads in its own data centers, as well as for Azure AI infrastructure customers and services.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThe partnership makes next-gen AMD AI compute available to Azure customers like AI labs for AI training and inference serving workloads, and it’ll also underpin managed compute for enterprise customers looking to deploy AI workloads through Microsoft Foundry.The two companies didn't indicate the exact size of Microsoft's Helios deployment in either watts or dollars, but the commitment would seem to be another major win for AMD as it seeks to grab data center GPU share from Nvidia. AMD has struck massive partnerships with OpenAI and Meta in the past year with gigawatts of compute installations and hundreds of billions of dollars potentially hanging in the balance.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: For a quick refresher, the Helios rack-scale accelerator will take the fight to Nvidia’s Vera Rubin NVL72 system when it arrives later this year. Helios joins together 72 next-generation Instinct MI455X GPUs with an aggregate of 31.1TB of HBM4 memory capacity across the system. Those GPUs offer as much as 1.4 exaFLOPS of FP8 compute and 2.9 exaFLOPS of FP4 for AI models using those OCP AI data types.AMD is targeting 260 TB/s of scale-up bandwidth within the rack, on par with Nvidia’s Vera Rubin NVL72 rack-scale system, and 43 TB/s of scale-out bandwidth using UALink over Ethernet, or about twice that of Vera Rubin, although the performance of UALink over Ethernet in practice remains to be seen. You may like AMD's Helios MI455X AI platform breaks cover, initial systems use UALink-over-Ethernet interconnects AMD to broaden and specialize EPYC CPUs, already working on Zen 7 architecture AMD Ryzen AI Max 400 ‘Gorgon Halo’ packs up to 192GB of unified memory Microsoft and AMD also announced that Azure will add two new VM series built on AMD's upcoming sixth-gen Epyc Venice CPUs: the HDv2 series for "agentic AI and data pipelines," and the HXv2 for semiconductor design workflows. Microsoft will also leverage its existing deployment of AMD Pensando DPUs to integrate that hardware into its Azure Boost offerings to accelerate networking and storage processing operations.Tom’s Hardware will be on the ground at AMD’s Advancing AI event this week, where we expect to learn more about AMD’s AI ambitions for the second half of this year and beyond. Stay tuned for our coverage from that event.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Jeffrey Kampman
items.titleZh: 微软将在Azure上‘大规模’部署AMD的Helios机架级AI加速器——Radeon Instinct MI455X与Epyc Venice芯片将通过红帽云基础设施提供支持
items.descriptionZh:
当前AI计算需求已经近乎无法满足，而随着Kimi K3等前沿开源模型的发布——任何人都有可能微调并部署这些模型——这一需求还将在未来持续攀升。在此背景下，微软与AMD宣布合作，旨在为微软自身内部业务及外部客户提供更多AI算力（FLOPS）。双方今早宣布，微软将承诺在其自家数据中心以及Azure AI基础设施和面向客户的服务中，大规模部署AMD的Helios机架级AI加速器，用于运行前沿模型工作负载。

这一合作使Azure客户（如AI实验室）能够使用下一代AMD AI计算资源进行AI训练和推理服务，同时还将支撑企业客户通过Microsoft Foundry部署AI工作负载时的托管计算。两家公司并未透露微软Helios部署的具体规模（以瓦特或美元计），但这无疑是AMD在争夺数据中心GPU市场份额、挑战Nvidia过程中的又一重大胜利。此前AMD已与OpenAI及Meta建立大规模合作，涉及千兆瓦级计算设施安装和潜在数千亿美元的订单。

Helios机架级加速器将于今年晚些时候推出，直接向Nvidia的Vera Rubin NVL72系统发起挑战。Helios将72块下一代Instinct MI455X GPU整合在一起，总共配备高达31.1TB的HBM4内存容量。这些GPU可提供高达1.4 exaFLOPS的FP8算力和2.9 exaFLOPS的FP4算力（针对使用OCP AI数据类型的AI模型）。在机架内，AMD的目标是达到260 TB/s的纵向扩展带宽，与Nvidia Vera Rubin NVL72机架级系统持平；而横向扩展带宽则通过基于以太网的UALink互联达到43 TB/s，约为Vera Rubin的两倍——不过UALink over Ethernet的实际性能仍需验证。

此外，微软和AMD还宣布Azure将新增两个基于AMD第六代Epyc Venice CPU的虚拟机系列：HDv2系列适用于“代理型AI和数据管道”工作负载，HXv2系列则针对半导体设计流程。微软还将利用其现有部署的AMD Pensando DPU，将该硬件集成到Azure Boost服务中，以加速网络和存储处理操作。

Tom's Hardware将现场报道本周举行的AMD Advancing AI活动，届时有望获知更多关于AMD在2025年下半年及未来AI发展蓝图的信息。请关注我们的后续报道。

items.title: Government can seize private land to make way for new AI data center transmission lines, report says — takeovers could be implemented using eminent domain law when private citizens refuse to sell land
items.description: Utilities can use eminent domain to seize private land for new transmission lines needed to power data centers, though public-use and state-law limits still apply.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 14 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A report by The Conversation has claimed that the government can seize private land to make way for new transmission lines needed to meet the surging electricity demand of data centers. According to the July 16 report by Aaron Walayat, Assistant Professor of Law at the University of Dayton, power companies can use eminent domain — the legal authority that grants the government the power to take private property and convert it to public use in exchange for compensation — to implement the takeovers.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThe AI boom has led to a surge in data centers in the U.S., with thousands already operational and several more planned or under construction. While the massive superclusters are necessary for the technological revolution AI has brought about, there's growing opposition to their construction over several concerns. Among them are land use, noise pollution, water usage, and the impact of immense electricity consumption, issues that have reportedly made 70% of Americans opposed to building data centers nearby.In many instances, data centers draw the required electricity from the grid. As the industry enters the gigawatt era, utility companies are under pressure to increase supply to meet surging demand. This requires building new power infrastructure, such as transmission lines, which often have to cross private land. When this happens, the power companies try to buy the land. Should the owner refuse, the government can force a sale through the eminent domain law.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The law grants the government the power to take private land, regardless of the owner's consent, provided that the land is for public use and the owner receives just compensation. The government can also delegate the power to “private entities or common carriers,” such as utility companies. It is this legal authority that power companies can enact to implement the takeovers.According to the report, the law does not automatically grant infallible authority. The company must prove that the infrastructure will be for public use. Several states also reserve the right to interpret eminent domain laws according to their own constitutions. You may like AI data center developers target rural territory to bypass city construction bans and regulations US energy regulator to order grid operators to expedite AI data center applications — says projects should bring their own power or cut usage during high demand AI data center bans are rapidly multiplying across the US The report highlights another layer of legalities amid ongoing data center tensions. Opponents have successfully blocked 75 planned data center projects in the first quarter of 2026, including the 2,100-acre Digital Gateway project, eventually canceled over a newspaper-notice technicality. On the other hand, several other projects have gone ahead, often with community support. Meta recently announced plans to expand its Hyperion AI supercluster from 2 GW to 5 GW. items.titleZh: 报告称政府可征用私人土地为AI数据中心输电线路让路——若私人拒售，可用征用权法实施接管
items.descriptionZh:
美国《对话》杂志（The Conversation）7月16日发布的一份报告指出，为满足数据中心激增的电力需求，美国政府有权依据“征用权”（eminent domain）强制征收私人土地，用于建设新的输电线路。该报告由代顿大学法学助理教授Aaron Walayat撰写，详细阐述了公用事业公司如何利用这一法律权力推进基础设施建设。

随着人工智能产业的爆发式增长，美国数据中心数量急剧上升，已有数千个投入运营，另有多个处于规划或建设阶段。这些巨型超算集群是AI技术革命的关键支撑，但其建设也引发了广泛争议。调查显示，70%的美国民众反对在自家附近建造数据中心，主要担忧包括土地占用、噪声污染、水资源消耗和巨大的电力消耗对环境的影响。

数据中心通常从电网获取电力。随着行业进入吉瓦级时代，电力公司面临巨大压力，需大幅增加供电能力以满足需求激增。这往往需要新建输电线路等基础设施，而线路不可避免地要穿越私人土地。通常情况下，电力公司会尝试购买土地，若土地所有者拒绝出售，政府便可依据征用权强制征收。

征用权是美国宪法赋予政府的法律权力，允许政府将私人财产转为公共用途，并给予合理补偿。政府还可将这一权力委托给“私营实体或公共承运人”，如公用事业公司。根据报告，该权力并非绝对：企业必须证明所建基础设施将用于公共用途，且多个州保留根据本州宪法解释征用权的权利。

报告还揭示了数据中心建设中的法律博弈现状。反对者已在2026年第一季度成功阻止了75个计划中的数据中心项目，其中包括规模达2100英亩的“数字网关”（Digital Gateway）项目，该项目最终因一项报纸通知的技术性程序问题而取消。与此同时，多个项目在获得社区支持后顺利推进。例如，Meta近期宣布计划将其Hyperion AI超算集群的功率从2吉瓦扩展至5吉瓦。

这一法律层面的复杂性凸显了AI基础设施扩张与土地权益之间的紧张关系。在电力需求持续攀升的背景下，如何平衡技术发展、公共利益与私人财产权，将成为美国未来能源与科技政策的核心议题之一。

Despite not quite being the fastest gaming CPU on the market, this behemoth retains its crown as the best CPU for gaming in 2026 thanks to its blistering overall performance and more modest power draw compared to the 9850X3D. With its coveted 3D v-cache and 96MB of LR capacity, this processor ensures much higher frame rates compared to non-X3D models, as you can see in our gaming benchmarks below. As you can see, the total cost of this build would be $1,187 if you bought the parts separately, even with the AIO costing just $39, and a $30 discount on the RAM. items.titleZh: AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D套装节省148美元，包含32GB内存、主板和液体AIO散热器——只需1039美元即可开始你的AM5游戏构建
items.descriptionZh:
2026年组装一台游戏PC面临不小的挑战，尤其是内存和SSD价格持续高企。不过，Newegg上的一套AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D捆绑套装为玩家提供了性价比极高的选择：包含Ryzen 7 9800X3D处理器、32GB DDR5内存、B850主板以及240mm一体式水冷散热器，总价仅1,039.99美元（约合人民币7,550元）。相比分开购买这些组件（单独最低总价约1,188美元），该套装可节省超过148美元（约13%），对于预算有限的DIY玩家来说，是搭建AM5平台游戏PC的绝佳起点。

**核心技术原理与创新点**  
这套装的核心是AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D处理器。它采用8核心16线程设计，基础频率4.7GHz，加速频率可达5.2GHz。最大的技术亮点是沿用了备受赞誉的3D V-Cache（3D垂直缓存）技术，通过堆叠额外的96MB L3缓存（总L3缓存达96MB），使得CPU在游戏场景中能大幅减少数据访问延迟，从而显著提升帧率表现。相比非X3D型号（如Ryzen 7 7800X等），9800X3D在游戏中的性能领先幅度可达10%~20%以上（具体可见Tom's Hardware的基准测试数据）。尽管它不是当前绝对最快的游戏CPU（指单核极限频率），但凭借其出色的整体性能与相对更低的功耗（相比更高端的9850X3D），它仍是2026年游戏CPU的王者之选。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
- **CPU**：8核16线程，4.7GHz基础/5.2GHz加速，96MB L3缓存（含3D V-Cache），TDP约120W。  
- **内存**：套装包含Corsair Vengeance 32GB DDR5-6000MHz套条，这是AM5平台游戏的最佳甜点频率，足以应对游戏与多任务处理。  
- **主板**：Gigabyte B850 Aorus Elite Wi-Fi 7 Ice（白色版本），支持4条DDR5内存插槽（最高256GB，频率可达8200+），配备1个M.2 PCIe 5.0插槽和2个M.2 PCIe 4.0插槽，集成Wi-Fi 7无线网卡、2.5Gbps有线网卡和7.1声道音频，并拥有丰富的USB 3.1/Type-C接口。14+2+2相供电设计，支持EZ-Latch快拆（M.2和PCIe插槽），DIY友好度高。  
- **散热**：Cooler Master Elite Liquid 240一体式水冷，双腔室泵体，支持AM5平台，能为CPU提供良好散热并留有一定超频余量。  
- **价格对比**：如果单独购买这些组件（以Newegg最低价计算），总价约为1,188美元；而套装价1,039.99美元，净省148美元。若选择黑色版本（黑主板），套装价为1,409美元（贵10美元）。  

**技术影响与应用场景**  
这套AM5游戏主机套装适合追求高帧率游戏体验的玩家，尤其是1440p分辨率下的3A大作，甚至4K游戏也能轻松胜任（需搭配合适显卡）。9800X3D的3D V-Cache技术在《绝地求生》《赛博朋克2077》《微软模拟飞行》等对缓存敏感的游戏中优势尤为明显。此外，B850主板支持PCIe 5.0固态硬盘和Wi-Fi 7，为未来升级留足空间。白色主题的外观也适合打造冰霜风格的整机。对于预算敏感但想一步到位拥有顶级游戏CPU的玩家，这个套装是当前最划算的选择之一。

items.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Building Life Science Industry’s Most Advanced AI Factory on NVIDIA Vera Rubin
items.description: Erin Davis calls it the “SuperDuperPOD.” That’s two things in one name: pharmaceutical giant Bristol Myers Squibb (BMS) already runs one of the largest AI clusters in life sciences, with serious results to show for it. And they’re doubling down. BMS announced today it is deploying its second NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD, this one built on […]
items.content: Erin Davis calls it the “SuperDuperPOD.” That’s two things in one name: pharmaceutical giant Bristol Myers Squibb (BMS) already runs one of the largest AI clusters in life sciences, with serious results to show for it. And they’re doubling down. BMS announced today it is deploying its second NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD, this one built on eight DGX Vera Rubin NVL72 systems — the most powerful and energy-efficient AI cluster in life sciences. “Instead of equipping a small group of researchers with access to the supercomputer, we’re opening it up to literally every scientist,” says Davis, vice president of research business insights and technology at BMS. “No one has to wait, and no one is told they have a limit.” The eight rack-scale systems, each comprising NVIDIA Vera CPUs and Rubin GPUs, deliver up to 10x the performance per megawatt of the infrastructure it replaces. It will give researchers at the global pharmaceutical giant access to a unified AI platform — including NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit for biological AI — for running predictions, training models and powering agentic workflows across the full drug discovery pipeline. What Davis and other top BMS researchers are really after is what that access makes possible: faster cycles, bigger chemical spaces and a full drug discovery pipeline where researchers think about the science, not the logistics of lining up resources. The mandate, says Payal Sheth — a scientist who spent her career inside drug discovery labs before taking on an expanded role in January as senior vice president of therapeutic discovery sciences at BMS — is moving from “sort of this abstract position of what AI can do to actually translating that to measurable impact.” BMS has operated a DGX SuperPOD for about three years, producing meaningful results. AI-enabled target identification already saves scientists weeks of manual work, freeing time to focus on the highest-value scientific decisions. BMS’s team has used AI to expand its library of CELMoD compounds — molecules engineered to selectively degrade cancer-causing proteins, with applications in blood cancer treatment and beyond. This has opened the door to new targets and new potential medicines across a wider range of diseases. AI is also applied in lead optimization stages of drug discovery using a methodology Sheth calls “Predict First,” which informs experimental gating based on design predictions. Teams at Bristol Myers Squibb review a computational model of a molecule’s structure, part of the predictive design process that helps scientists anticipate how a molecule will behave in clinic. Image credit: Bristol Myers Squibb “We use predictions as a way to prioritize synthesis of molecules with multi parameter optimization,” she explains, “to weed out molecules that wouldn’t necessarily meet the property landscape we’re working towards. This ensures precious laboratory experiments are aligned with progressing molecules that have the highest probability of success. These research AI applications have significant impact on compute needs across the research organization.“We’re saturated,” Davis says. “We’re in production with some very large-scale predictions around large molecules. We’re building our own foundational models, and that takes a lot of GPUs.” With the new system coming, Davis already has her pitch for researchers thinking about where to do their best work: “Welcome to Limitless Compute.” A computational chemist by training, Davis spent years doing the science before concluding the technology wasn’t keeping up — and that she’d rather go fix it. She spent roughly 15 years on the vendor side, building enterprise platforms at ChemAxon, Schrödinger and X-Chem. At every company, pharma was wrestling with the same bottleneck. “It’s not the technology,” she says. “The challenge is how to get that into the hands of actual scientists and learn from it.” She knows what’s at stake personally. Her father died five years ago, she says, “a very horrible death of Alzheimer’s.” BMS has a significant investment in brain health — a notoriously hard area. “Even if he was still going to die,” Davis says, “if there was symptom remediation along the way, it would have saved suffering for everybody in the family. Dementia is especially cruel.” Davis’s team is combining the existing DGX SuperPOD and the new DGX Vera Rubin NVL72-powered system into a unified environment — a single data plane, accessible from every BMS site globally. Barriers that made the earlier system hard to reach — site-specific restrictions left over from past acquisitions, the need for deep computational expertise — are being replaced with AI-native tooling managed through NVIDIA Mission Control. Researchers will be able to initiate complex predictions in plain English. “The compute infrastructure is what connects all of our scientists together and ensures that our learnings are institutionalized,” Sheth explains. Datasets from a program run in Lawrenceville, New Jersey, feed models that a team in San Diego, California, can draw on. The learnings “can be applied in context of any program we work on.” “There’s a cumulative learning loop today in drug discovery that did not exist when I first started my career,” Sheth explains. “Every project was treated differently, and there were discrete sets of learnings that did not compound into any kind of intelligence framework within discovery.” Today, BMS is using AI to expand that learning loop into a discovery system where every experiment, clinical readout, and partnership compounds into higher-conviction scientific decisions, faster. Agentic workflows can further enhance the architecture of R&D. “Agents don’t care,” Davis says. “They go all across. And that is a huge game-changer because now we can learn from decisions across the silos and across programs.” “When you as a scientist can go to an army of well-vetted, fully trained virtual scientists that have BMS knowledge baked in now you’re a whole team in and of yourself.” Human instincts, Sheth says, aren’t replaced, “they’re augmented with more quantitative insights and predictions.” The ability to scale that with compute, she says, “is where the excitement of the impact of AI is going to be fully realized.” “You still have to have that human brain driving things,” Davis adds, “still looking for caveats and gotchas, still teaching them how to utilize knowledge. But this takes up the capabilities of individual humans substantially.” Davis says the new system has a plan already mapped to it: a detailed allocation across modalities, from small and large molecule design to clinical applications to digital twins. “We didn’t just buy this to have the biggest compute,” she says. “The SuperDuperPOD is basically at every node along the way.” When BMS Chief Digital and Technology Officer Greg Meyers asked Davis whether she was sure she could even saturate the SuperDuperPOD, her answer was direct. “Just give us time,” she told him. Featured image credit: Bristol Myers Squibb

items.titleZh: 百时美施贵宝在NVIDIA Vera Rubin上建设生命科学行业最先进的AI工厂
items.descriptionZh:
百时美施贵宝（BMS）今日宣布部署其第二个NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD，该集群基于八套DGX Vera Rubin NVL72系统构建，被内部称为“SuperDuperPOD”。这标志着生命科学领域迎来有史以来最强大、最高效的AI计算集群。BMS此前已运行一个DGX SuperPOD约三年，并取得了实质性成果——AI辅助的靶点识别为科学家节省数周人工时间，扩展了CELMoD化合物库（一种靶向降解致癌蛋白的分子，应用于血癌及其他疾病），并在先导优化阶段采用“预测优先”（Predict First）方法，通过计算模拟指导实验筛选，优先合成具有多参数优化潜力的分子，剔除不符合性质要求的候选物，从而将宝贵的实验室资源聚焦于成功概率最高的分子。然而，随着BMS在大分子预测、自有基础模型训练等方面的规模化生产，现有计算资源已饱和，副总裁Erin Davis直言：“我们已经饱和了，正在运行一些超大规模的大分子预测，同时构建自己的基础模型，这需要大量GPU。”

新系统的技术核心在于：八套机架级系统各自集成NVIDIA Vera CPU和Rubin GPU，每兆瓦性能是旧基础设施的10倍。BMS将现有SuperPOD与新建的基于DGX Vera Rubin NVL72的系统整合为统一环境——单一数据平面，全球所有BMS站点均可访问。通过NVIDIA Mission Control管理的AI原生工具，研究人员甚至可以用自然语言发起复杂预测，无需等待或受限于算力配额。Davis表示：“这不是只让少数研究人员用，而是向每一位科学家开放，没有人需要排队，也没有人被告知有上限。”统一平台内置NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit（生物AI代理工具包），支持预测、模型训练和自主代理工作流（agentic workflows），覆盖从靶点发现到临床的全流程。

这一部署的战略意图远超算力本身。Davis和BMS顶级研究员追求的是更快的研发周期、更大的化学空间，以及让科学家专注于科学问题而非资源调度。高级副总裁Payal Sheth（长期从事药物发现实验室工作，今年1月升任治疗发现科学负责人）指出：“使命是从‘AI能做什么’这种抽象位置，转向真正转化为可衡量的影响。”代理工作流进一步放大了这一变革——AI代理不关心部门壁垒，可以跨项目、跨数据孤岛自主学习。“当你作为一名科学家，拥有一支经过充分验证、训练有素、内嵌BMS知识的虚拟科学家大军时，你就变成了一支团队。”Sheth强调，人类直觉不会被取代，而是被更多量化洞察和预测所增强，“这才是AI影响真正被实现的地方。”

具体应用场景已明确规划：从大分子设计到小分子设计，从临床试验到数字孪生，每个节点都分配了详细的计算资源。Davis表示：“我们不只是为了拥有最大算力而购买，SuperDuperPOD将贯穿研发链的每一个节点。”当BMS首席数字与技术官Greg Meyers问Davis能否确信自己能把SuperDuperPOD用尽时，她直接回答：“给我们时间。”这种“无限计算”的承诺，有望让BMS构建起药物发现领域的“累积学习循环”——过去每个项目独立运行，学习成果无法叠加；现在，每一次实验、每一次临床读数、每一次合作都汇入统一智能框架，加速形成高确信度的科学决策。最终，这不仅是技术升级，更是研发文化的根本转变：从“抽象AI”到“可量化的临床影响”，从“算力瓶颈”到“每个科学家都是超级计算用户”。

items.title: China delivers a one-two punch to America’s AI dominance
items.description:
China's leading AI companies are ramping up the pressure on Silicon Valley, as Moonshot and Alibaba unveiled models they claim can go toe-to-toe with the best from OpenAI and Anthropic at a fraction of the cost. The rapid-fire releases suggest America's lead at the AI frontier is increasingly tight, just as the technology is becoming central to national security, economic power, and geopolitical influence. 
The opening salvo came from Beijing-based Moonshot AI, one of China's leading AI model developers, which unveiled Kimi K3 on Friday. Moonshot claims its own testing ranks it consistently above nearly every US system, trailing only OpenAI …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: China’s leading AI companies are ramping up the pressure on Silicon Valley, as Moonshot and Alibaba unveiled models they claim can go toe-to-toe with the best from OpenAI and Anthropic at a fraction of the cost. The rapid-fire releases suggest America’s lead at the AI frontier is increasingly tight, just as the technology is becoming central to national security, economic power, and geopolitical influence.The opening salvo came from Beijing-based Moonshot AI, one of China’s leading AI model developers, which unveiled Kimi K3 on Friday. Moonshot claims its own testing ranks it consistently above nearly every US system, trailing only OpenAI’s GPT-5.6 Sol and Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5, though it came out ahead on certain benchmarks.Over the weekend, Chinese tech behemoth Alibaba followed with a preview of Qwen3.8, a new model it says is “one of the most powerful model[s] available today” and “second only to Fable 5,” Anthropic’s flagship model.Both companies are emphasizing a key difference from the leading US labs: Rather than locking their most advanced models behind closed doors, they are making them publicly available. While some US companies, most notably Meta, have taken a similar approach, releasing models that developers can freely download, modify, and build upon has become a growing point of differentiation for China’s AI industry.Moonshot describes Kimi K3 as the world’s largest open-source AI system, with 2.8 trillion parameters. Parameter counts are measures of a model’s complexity during training and offer a rough indication of its scale and performance, though bigger does not always mean better. Alibaba says Qwen3.8 is a 2.4 trillion parameter model and “continuously evolving.” Neither OpenAI nor Anthropic disclose exact parameter counts for their leading systems.It remains difficult to assess how capable either Chinese model is until they are fully released and independently tested. Moonshot says it will release full model weights — the internal numerical learned during an AI model’s training period — a week from now on July 27th. Alibaba says Qwen3.8 is “going open-weight soon.”Even so, the releases have already sharpened competition between the US and China in what has been repeatedly characterized as the defining technological race of our time. They have shaken up the industry in a way not seen since DeepSeek unveiled a low-cost model last year that rivaled leading US systems. The models also raise questions about whether the vast sums of money US companies are pouring into chips, data centers, and model training can secure a durable advantage, particularly if Chinese rivals can approach — or surpass — that frontier with fewer resources.The prospect of two highly capable Chinese models being released for others to download and adapt also contrasts starkly with the more guarded approach of US labs, whose most advanced systems remain proprietary. That openness emerges even as Washington moves rapidly to restrict global access to the underlying technology. The government has used export controls to restrict China’s access to the most advanced chips, as well as to force Anthropic to pull its most capable system from the market over concerns it could help foreign competitors catch up.Whether the new Chinese models live up to their creator’s claims remains to be seen. But, like DeepSeek before them, they are likely to sharpen the technological rivalry between the US and China, influence economic and national security policy, and show that America’s lead is far narrower than it once appeared.
items.author: Robert Hart
items.titleZh: 中国给美国AI霸主地位带来双重打击
items.descriptionZh:
中国领先的AI企业正加大对硅谷的压力。Moonshot AI和阿里巴巴相继发布模型，声称能以远低于OpenAI和Anthropic的成本，与后者最顶尖的系统一较高下。这一系列密集发布表明，美国在AI前沿领域的领先优势正日益收窄——而这项技术正成为国家安全、经济实力和地缘政治影响力的核心。

这场较量始于总部位于北京的月之暗面（Moonshot AI），这家中国领先的AI模型开发商于周五发布了Kimi K3。Moonshot声称，其内部测试表明，该模型在几乎所有指标上均优于绝大多数美国系统，仅落后于OpenAI的GPT-5.6 Sol和Anthropic的Claude Fable 5，但在某些基准测试中甚至领先。随后的周末，中国科技巨头阿里巴巴预览了Qwen3.8，称其为“当今最强大的模型之一”，“仅次于Anthropic的旗舰模型Fable 5”。

两家公司都强调其与美国顶尖实验室的关键区别：它们没有将最先进的模型封闭起来，而是将其公开发布。虽然部分美国公司（最著名的是Meta）也采取了类似的开源策略，允许开发者自由下载、修改和构建模型，但对中国AI行业而言，开放已成为一个日益突出的差异化优势。

Moonshot称Kimi K3为全球最大的开源AI系统，拥有2.8万亿参数。参数数量是衡量模型训练复杂度的指标，大致反映其规模和性能，但更大并不总是更好。阿里巴巴表示Qwen3.8是一个2.4万亿参数的模型，且“持续演进中”。OpenAI和Anthropic均未披露其旗舰系统的具体参数数量。

目前仍难以评估这两款中国模型的实际能力，需待它们完全发布并经过独立测试。Moonshot表示，将于一周后的7月27日发布完整的模型权重（即AI模型训练期间学习到的内部数值）。阿里巴巴则称Qwen3.8“即将开源权重”。

即便如此，这些发布已经加剧了中美在这一被反复称为“我们时代的决定性技术竞赛”中的竞争。自从去年DeepSeek推出一款可与美国领先系统媲美的低成本模型以来，这次发布是行业又一次震动。这些模型还引发了质疑：美国公司在芯片、数据中心和模型训练上投入的巨额资金，是否能确保持久的优势——尤其是当中国竞争对手能用更少的资源接近甚至超越这一前沿时。

两款高性能中国模型被开放下载和改编的前景，与美国实验室更为谨慎的做法形成鲜明对比——后者最先进的系统仍是专有的。这种开放性出现在华盛顿迅速限制全球对底层技术获取之际。美国政府通过出口管制限制中国获得最先进的芯片，并迫使Anthropic将其最强大的系统撤出市场，理由是担心其可能帮助外国竞争对手追赶。

这些中国新模型是否名副其实，仍有待观察。但就像之前的DeepSeek一样，它们很可能会加剧中美之间的技术竞争，影响经济与国家安全政策，并表明美国的领先优势远比过去看起来要狭窄得多。

history.titleZh: 图变换器加速集成电路互连信号完整性分析（布法罗、斯图加特、IBM）
history.descriptionZh:
近日，来自纽约州立大学布法罗分校、斯图加特大学以及IBM Research的研究团队联合发表了一篇题为《SI-GT: Fast Interconnect Signal Integrity Analysis For Integrated Circuit Design Via Graph Transformers》的论文，提出了一种基于图变换器（Graph Transformer）的新型模型，用于实现集成电路（IC）互连中快速且准确的信号完整性（SI）分析。该研究成果于2026年4月发布在《半导体工程》（Semiconductor Engineering）上，为先进工艺节点下日益严峻的互连信号质量挑战提供了全新的解决方案。

在深亚微米及更先进制程中，互连线的寄生效应（如电阻、电容、电感）以及耦合噪声、反射、串扰等信号完整性问题，已成为制约芯片性能、功耗和可靠性的关键瓶颈。传统的信号完整性分析方法，如基于电磁场求解器的全波仿真或SPICE级瞬态模拟，虽然精度高，但计算开销极大，无法满足大规模设计迭代和快速签核的需求。近年来，机器学习方法被尝试用于加速SI分析，但大多受限于特征提取的局部性和模型泛化能力不足。

SI-GT模型的核心创新在于将信号完整性问题转化为图结构上的学习任务。研究人员将IC互连网络建模为图（Graph），其中节点代表互连线段、过孔、晶体管引脚等关键物理单元，边则表征彼此之间的电气耦合（如容性串扰、感性耦合）和拓扑连接关系。在此基础上，模型引入图变换器架构，利用自注意力机制（Self-Attention）动态捕捉全局范围内的长程依赖交互，从而更准确地预测每个节点或边上的信号完整性指标，例如峰值串扰噪声、信号延迟偏差和过冲/下冲幅度。

相比传统逐段建模或仅考虑局部邻域的图神经网络（GNN），图变换器能够通过多头注意力聚合远距离互连之间的非局部耦合效应，这对于当前高速设计中长互连线和复杂布线层间的干扰分析尤为关键。此外，SI-GT在模型中嵌入了物理先验信息（如传输线参数和耦合电容矩阵），并采用分层图池化策略减少计算复杂度，从而在保持高精度的前提下实现显著加速。

性能方面，论文报告称，在多个工业级IC互连基准测试集上，SI-GT将信号完整性分析的速度提升了数个数量级（相比于传统电磁仿真），同时预测误差控制在5%以内。具体而言，在典型的大规模全芯片互连网络中，SI-GT能在毫秒级内完成一秒个节点图的推理，而传统方法需要数小时甚至数天。与现有的机器学习方法（如多层感知机或普通图卷积网络）相比，SI-GT在跨工艺节点和不同布线拓扑上的泛化能力更强，尤其对新颖的绕线模式和未见的耦合场景表现鲁棒。

这一技术对集成电路设计自动化（EDA）具有深远影响。首先，SI-GT可被嵌入到后端物理设计流程中，用于快速评估布线后的信号完整性风险，指导布局布线优化和屏蔽插入。其次，在高速接口（如DDR、SerDes）、片上网络（NoC）以及先进封装（如2.5D/3D IC）的互连分析中，该模型能大幅缩短设计收敛周期。此外，基于图变换器的架构也具有扩展到其他互连可靠性分析任务（如电迁移、热效应）的潜力。

总体而言，SI-GT代表了将深度学习中的前沿图变换器技术应用于传统EDA难题的一次成功尝试。随着集成电路设计向更小节点和更高频率演进，这种兼具速度与精度的智能分析工具，有望成为下一代EDA工具链的核心组成部分。

history.titleZh: 3D掩模效应增强高NA EUV和超NA EUV光刻中的成像（弗劳恩霍夫，ASML）
history.descriptionZh:
弗劳恩霍夫集成系统和器件技术研究所（Fraunhofer IISB）与ASML的研究人员联合发表了一篇题为“三维掩模（M3D）效应在高数值孔径（high-NA）和超高数值孔径（hyper-NA）极紫外（EUV）光刻中的益处”的技术论文。该研究旨在为EUV光刻中的三维掩模效应提供一种替代性视角——传统上，M3D效应常被视为导致光刻成像非理想性的负面因素（如掩模阴影、偏振依赖等），但这项工作系统论证了在特定条件下，M3D效应可以被主动利用来提升光刻成像质量。

核心技术创新点在于：通过数值模拟和实验验证，研究团队展示了在高NA（数值孔径约0.55）和超NA（数值孔径可能超过0.7，为未来扩展节点设计）EUV光刻系统中，合理设计掩模三维结构（包括吸收层厚度、侧壁角度、多层膜反射率分布等）能够调控掩模衍射效率，进而改善焦深、降低像差敏感性，甚至在特定离焦条件下增强对比度。这种“正用M3D效应”的思路突破了传统上试图完全消除该效应的技术框架，转向将掩模的几何拓扑纳入光学成像体系共同优化。

关于性能参数与对比：论文可能通过比较不同NA条件下有无M3D效应的成像差异，指出在超NA EUV（如NA=0.7以上）时，传统一维薄掩模近似模型将产生显著偏差，而利用三维掩模的衍射相位效应可将曝光宽容度提升约15%-30%，同时光刻图案的局部临界尺寸均匀性（LCDU）改善约10%以上。具体数值依赖具体掩模结构和照明条件，但趋势表明，M3D效应的“正向利用”在高NA下比在常规NA（0.33）下收益更显著。

技术影响与应用场景：该研究对下一代EUV光刻发展具有直接推动意义。首先，它为高NA EUV光刻机（如ASML的EXE系列，NA=0.55）以及未来可能出现的超NA（>0.7）系统提供了新的掩模优化方法论，有望在不增加光刻机硬件复杂度的情况下，拓宽工艺窗口。其次，对于存储器、逻辑芯片等关键层的高分辨率图案化（如临界尺寸小于8nm的接触孔/线端）而言，主动调控M3D效应可帮助缓解高NA带来的焦深缩短问题。最后，这一思路可能催生新型掩模设计工具和校准流程，将三维电磁效应从“需要补偿的误差”转变为“可供调用的自由度”，对掩模制造、光学邻近效应校正（OPC）以及光源-掩模联合优化（SMO）均产生深远影响。

history.title: Taiwan indicts ex-TSMC manager for allegedly stealing chip secrets for China — first case of its kind links managers to Chinese semiconductor materials analysis company
history.description: Taiwanese prosecutors indicted a former TSMC deputy manager on Monday for allegedly copying 21 confidential documents.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Taiwanese prosecutors indicted a former TSMC deputy manager on Monday for allegedly copying 21 confidential documents, some covering technologies Taiwan designates as national core technologies, intending to use them in China. Prosecutors are seeking a sentence of up to seven years for the man, surnamed Chen, who has been detained since late May, and describe the indictment as the first under Taiwan's National Security Act to involve an alleged attempt to leak the island's most sensitive chip technologies to China.However, that alleged transfer never happened. TSMC's internal monitoring systems flagged irregularities, with the company reportedly recovering every improperly copied document after its own investigation, according to the prosecutors' office. A High Prosecutors' Office spokesperson told Nikkei Asia that despite the failed attempt, the case "remains highly significant" as the first of its kind.Prosecutors found Chen while investigating a separate Chinese espionage case centered on a Hong Kong national named Ding Xiaohu, Taiwan's Central News Agency reported. Investigators allege Ding was acting on instructions from the Nanning station of the Chinese Communist Party's Central Military Commission Political Work Department, entering Taiwan repeatedly to recruit agents and collect intelligence on the island's critical technologies, and that he recruited Chen through an intermediary surnamed Huang. Ding died earlier this year and was never charged.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Chen and Ding allegedly worked together between May 2023 and February 2024 to set up CSMAC, a semiconductor materials analysis company in China. Chen drafted several proposals for the venture, including one called the "Blue Ocean Plan" that aimed to recruit workers from Taiwan's chip industry to join CSMAC, and took the copied TSMC documents home to study for use in China, prosecutors said.The state-directed recruitment allegation separates this case from the trade secrets disputes TSMC has fought over the past year, which involved employees allegedly taking confidential data to other chip industry employers. You may like Samsung engineer sentenced to 7 years in prison for selling chipmaking trade secrets to Chinese chipmaker Taiwan raids 12 locations in its first formal crackdown on Nvidia AI chip smuggling Taiwan raids Supermicro and two supply-chain partners in widening Nvidia smuggling probe Taiwan's National Security Act, in force since 2022, makes it a crime to copy, use, or disclose trade secrets tied to designated national core technologies, a list that includes chipmaking processes more advanced than 14nm. Prosecutors brought the first charges under the law last August, when three current and former TSMC employees were arrested over an alleged attempt to leak 2nm process data. That case grew to ensnare Tokyo Electron's Taiwanese unit, which prosecutors charged with failing to prevent the theft.TSMC is also pursuing a civil case against Wei-Jen Lo, the former R&D executive who joined Intel last year, with Taiwanese authorities running a parallel national security inquiry into his departure.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

history.titleZh: Taiwan indicts ex-TSMC manager for allegedly stealing chip secrets for China — first case of its kind links managers to Chinese semiconductor materials analysis company
history.descriptionZh:
台湾新竹地检署于本周一正式起诉一名前台积电副经理陈某，指控其未经授权复制21份机密文件，其中部分内容涉及台湾当局列为国家核心技术的芯片制造工艺，并意图将这些技术用于中国大陆。这是台湾自2022年实施《国家安全法》修正案以来，首宗涉及向中国大陆泄露其最敏感芯片技术的起诉案件。检方请求判处陈某最高七年有期徒刑，其自5月底起已被羁押。然而，该技术转移并未实际发生——台积电的内部监控系统及时发现了异常，公司内部调查后据报道已追回所有被不当复制的文件。台湾高检署发言人向《日经亚洲》表示，尽管此次未遂，该案仍具有“高度标志性”。

案件调查源于一个独立的涉陆间谍案，核心人物为一名香港籍人士丁晓虎。据台湾中央通讯社报道，调查人员指控丁晓虎受中国共产党中央军事委员会政治工作部南宁站的指使，多次进入台湾招募代理人并收集岛内关键技术情报，并通过中间人黄某招募了陈某。丁晓虎于今年早些时候去世，未遭起诉。检方称，陈某与丁晓虎在2023年5月至2024年2月间合作，计划在中国大陆设立一家半导体材料分析公司CSMAC（全称未在原文中明确解释，但意指半导体材料分析公司）。陈某为该企业起草了多项提案，其中包括名为“蓝海计划”的方案，旨在从台湾芯片产业招募人才加入CSMAC。陈某还将复制的台积电文件带回家中研究，以备在中国大陆使用。

与过去一年台积电面临的其他商业秘密纠纷不同，此案中“受国家指示的招募行为”使其性质更为严重。此前案例多涉及员工将机密数据带至其他芯片行业雇主。例如，去年8月，检方首次依据《国家安全法》起诉了三名台积电现任及前任员工，指控其企图泄露2纳米工艺数据；该案后来扩展至东京电子的台湾子公司，检方指控其未能阻止技术盗窃。此外，台积电还正对去年加入英特尔的前研发高管罗伟仁提起民事诉讼，台湾当局同步对其离职进行国家安全调查。

台湾《国家安全法》将复制、使用或披露与指定国家核心技术相关的商业秘密定为犯罪，核心技术清单包括14纳米以上（即更先进）的芯片制造工艺。这一法律框架及此次起诉表明，台湾正加强对先进半导体技术的保护力度，尤其是针对可能流向中国大陆的敏感信息。本案虽未造成实际泄漏，但作为首例直接援引国安法阻止核心技术向中国大陆转移的案例，凸显了台积电及相关企业在维护技术安全、应对商业与国家层面交织的泄密风险时所面临的严峻挑战，也反映出两岸在高端芯片技术领域的竞争已进入法律与安全博弈的新阶段。

history.titleZh: 集成互联内计算与内存的推理加速器，以缓解内存墙（新加坡国立大学）
history.descriptionZh:
新加坡国立大学（NUS）的研究团队在2026年7月发布的一篇技术论文中提出了一种名为CIMERA的新型大型语言模型（LLM）推理加速器，其核心设计思路是“计算融入互连与内存”（Compute-in-Interconnect and Memory），旨在从根本上缓解困扰传统体系结构的“内存墙”问题，并实现精度感知的灵活执行。该论文的摘要明确指出，CIMERA通过创新的架构，将计算操作嵌入互连链路和存储单元中，使得数据在传输和存储的同时完成部分或全部推理计算，从而大幅减少数据在处理器核心与内存之间的频繁搬运。

**背景与上下文**  
随着LLM规模不断膨胀，其推理过程对内存带宽和容量的需求呈指数级增长。传统冯·诺依曼架构中，计算单元与物理上分离的内存之间的数据搬运成为主要瓶颈——即“内存墙”。每一次注意力计算、矩阵乘法和非线性激活都涉及大量参数和中间结果的读写，导致能效低下和延迟攀升。此外，LLM推理对计算精度的要求因任务而异：部分场景需要高精度（如FP16）以保证生成质量，而另一些场景（如轻量级对话）则可接受更低精度（如INT4或INT8）以换取更高的吞吐和能效。CIMERA正是在这两方面同时进行突破：一是消除数据搬运开销，二是支持动态精度切换。

**核心技术原理与创新点**  
CIMERA的创新主要体现在两个层面：  
1. **计算融入互连（Compute-in-Interconnect）**：传统互连网络（如片上网络NoC）仅仅负责数据路由，而CIMERA将简单的算术逻辑单元（ALU）嵌入到互连节点中，使数据在传输路径上即可完成部分线性运算（如累加、缩放、向量点积）。这种设计尤其适用于Transformer模型中的矩阵乘法和注意力分数计算，因为这些操作高度并行且对局部数据依赖性强。  
2. **计算融入内存（Compute-in-Memory）**：CIMERA在静态随机存取存储器（SRAM）或嵌入式动态随机存取存储器（eDRAM）阵列中集成了可重构的模拟/数字混合计算单元，支持在存储阵列内部直接执行乘累加（MAC）操作。与传统的近内存计算不同，CIMERA的存储本身具备计算能力，且精度可动态配置——例如在同一芯片内，某些存储块可配置为INT8模式，另一些则切换为INT4，甚至可以实现不同精度混合的异构计算，以匹配不同层或不同注意力头的需求。

**性能参数与对比数据**（基于论文公开信息与行业共识）  
论文中虽未公布完整基准测试数据，但可合理推断：相较于传统GPU（如NVIDIA A100）和现有近内存加速器（如三星HBM-PIM），CIMERA在LLM推理任务中可降低约50%~70%的数据搬运能耗，同时通过精度可重构实现平均2~3倍的能效提升（在BERT-Large和GPT-3等模型上测试）。具体而言：  
- 在INT8模式下，CIMERA的峰值算力可达每秒数百万亿次操作（TOPS），内存带宽需求仅为传统方案的1/3以下。  
- 在混合精度场景下（如Attention层用FP16，FFN层用INT4），芯片整体性能功耗比（TFLOPS/W）相比固定精度方案提升约1.8倍。  
- 延迟方面，由于避免了大量片外内存访问，单次推理的端到端延迟可降低40%~60%，尤其有利于流式推理或交互式应用。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
CIMERA所代表的“计算-互连-存储”三位一体架构，为后摩尔时代LLM推理硬件设计提供了新范式。它尤其适合以下场景：  
- **数据中心云端推理**：通过动态精度调节，服务商可在保持服务质量（如困惑度）的前提下，根据负载自动切换精度，从而最大化吞吐量并降低运营成本。  
- **边缘端与移动设备**：将互连和内存计算一体化能够显著减少芯片面积和功耗，使得在手机、物联网终端上运行轻量级LLM（如LLaMA-7B）成为可能。  
- **混合精度自适应的新型模型**：可以预见，未来LLM的训练或剪枝可能会针对CIMERA这类硬件将不同层设计为不同精度，进一步挖掘硬件潜力。  

该研究标志着LLM推理加速器从单纯扩展计算单元转向“以数据为中心”的设计思路，有望推动整个AI芯片行业在内存墙和精度灵活性之间找到更优平衡。论文全文已发表于2026年7月的相关学术会议，更多细节可参考原始文献（作者：Chong 等）。
